content: Pharmaceutical pricing mechanisms vary globally: (1) External reference pricing - prices benchmarked against other countries (most EU nations); (2) Internal reference pricing - drugs grouped by therapeutic class with common reimbursement level; (3) Price-volume agreements - rebates if sales exceed thresholds; (4) Free pricing with negotiation - US system where PBMs negotiate confidential rebates; (5) Government price controls - direct setting of maximum prices. Pharmacy Benefit Managers (PBMs) in the US act as intermediaries between manufacturers, insurers, and pharmacies, negotiating rebates (often 20-50% of list price) but criticized for opaque practices and favoring high-list-price drugs.
